allies.rationale_text: Anchoring supports endowment-effect because owned assets become reference points that distort later valuation and comparison.
allies.source_quote: anchored to the past status quo

allies.rationale_text: Rationalization amplifies endowment-effect when owners spin persuasive stories to justify a value that their attachment is already inflating.
allies.source_quote: This self-focused perspective can engage motivated reasoning, where individuals process information specifically to get a conclusion we want.

antagonists.rationale_text: Sunk-cost-fallacy conflicts with endowment-effect by worsening the tendency to hold owned assets long after their value or usefulness has deteriorated.
antagonists.source_quote: endowment-effect conflicts with sunk-cost-fallacy when owned assets are overvalued and held despite deteriorating value.

antagonists.rationale_text: Status-quo-bias conflicts with endowment-effect when attachment to current holdings suppresses rational switching or divestment.
antagonists.source_quote: endowment-effect conflicts with status-quo-bias when attachment to current holdings suppresses rational switching.

antagonists.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking counters endowment-effect by forcing a re-evaluation of owned assets based on underlying facts instead of ownership-derived value.
antagonists.source_quote: endowment-effect conflicts with first-principles-thinking when ownership inflates perceived value beyond what the underlying facts justify.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Endowment-effect conflicts with sunk-cost-fallacy when owned assets are overvalued and held despite deteriorating value.
structured_tensions.source_quote: endowment-effect vs sunk-cost-fallacy: endowment-effect conflicts with sunk-cost-fallacy (sunk cost fallacy) when owned assets are overvalued and held despite deteriorating value.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Endowment-effect conflicts with status-quo-bias when attachment to current holdings suppresses rational switching.
structured_tensions.source_quote: endowment-effect vs status-quo-bias: endowment-effect conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when attachment to current holdings suppresses rational switching.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Endowment-effect conflicts with first-principles-thinking when ownership inflates perceived value beyond what the underlying facts justify.
structured_tensions.source_quote: endowment-effect vs first-principles-thinking: endowment-effect conflicts with first-principles-thinking (first principles thinking) when ownership inflates perceived value beyond what the underlying facts justify, and stripping assumptions away is resisted.

curation_notes.summary: The retained relation surface stays very close to the source: ownership anchoring, rationalization pressure, and three explicit structured tensions that cleanly fit current ids.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader System 1 or framing expansion because the current vocabulary already supports a cleaner valuation-distortion surface.
open_questions: Opportunity-cost is source-real, but the explicit structured-tension surface was stronger and cleaner on sunk-cost-fallacy, status-quo-bias, and first-principles-thinking.
